metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Correct the 'no inbound links' premise: replace or update the ~9 src/ module docstrings (`composer.py`, `leaf.py`, `_seam.py`, `unlink.py`, `__init__.py`, `_lib_writes.py`, `validate.py`, `next_batch.py`, `lock.py`) and `README.md:604` plus test file headers that reference `docs/bash-migration.md`, either removing the references or redirecting them to `docs/architecture.md` before deleting the file.
findings.finding: The plan claims `docs/bash-migration.md` has 'no inbound links', but grep reveals at least 9 inbound references in production source files (`src/rebar/_commands/composer.py:1`, `leaf.py:1`, `_seam.py:1`, `unlink.py:1`, `__init__.py:54`, `_lib_writes.py:308`, `_engine_support/validate.py:15`, `_engine_support/next_batch.py:11`, `_store/lock.py:7`) plus `README.md:604` and test-file docstrings. The remediation only asks to check `docs/architecture.md` for a pointer, but `architecture.md` has no such pointer — the real inbound refs are in `src/` and `tests/`, which the plan does not address. AC1 ('no dangling references to it') would be violated by every one of these src/ references.
findings.location: Finding section: 'no inbound links'; Remediation: 'confirm/remove any `docs/architecture.md` pointer'; AC: `docs/bash-migration.md` deleted; no dangling references to it
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the remediation: 'grep -r bash-migration.md src/ tests/ README.md and update/remove each reference before deleting the file; note docs/architecture.md has no such pointer and needs no change.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Enumerate and address all unenumerated inbound references to docs/bash-migration.md outside docs/ (README.md:604, 4 test module docstrings, 6 src/_commands docstrings, src/_store/lock.py:7, src/_lib_writes.py:308, src/_engine_support/validate.py and next_batch.py) before the file is deleted to satisfy AC #1.
findings.finding: The plan asserts bash-migration.md has 'no inbound links', but grep reveals at least 14 files outside docs/ that reference it: README.md:604, tests/integration/test_store_concurrency.py:1, tests/interfaces/queries/test_next_batch_compute.py:1, tests/interfaces/queries/test_validate_compute.py:1, tests/interfaces/facades/test_leaf_writes_parity.py:1, src/rebar/_commands/composer.py:1, src/rebar/_commands/leaf.py:1, src/rebar/_commands/__init__.py:54, src/rebar/_commands/unlink.py:1, src/rebar/_commands/_seam.py:1, src/rebar/_store/lock.py:7, src/rebar/_lib_writes.py:308, src/rebar/_engine_support/validate.py:15, src/rebar/_engine_support/next_batch.py:11. AC #1 requires 'no dangling references', but these consumers are not enumerated in the plan.
findings.location: Finding section: 'docs/bash-migration.md (STATUS: COMPLETE, no inbound links)'; AC #1: 'no dangling references to it'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a remediation sub-step: for each reference, either remove the citation, replace it with a pointer to the relevant surviving doc (e.g. docs/architecture.md or docs/concurrency.md), or note it is a historical reference that can be deleted safely. The test docstrings in particular can simply drop the §-section citations.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Expand the Remediation's dangling-reference sweep to enumerate the actual inbound link set: README.md:604, nine src/rebar/ module docstrings (_lib_writes, _engine_support/validate, _engine_support/next_batch, _store/lock, _commands/composer, _commands/leaf, _commands/__init__, _commands/unlink, _commands/_seam), and three test-file docstrings (test_store_concurrency, test_next_batch_compute, test_leaf_writes_parity) — not just docs/architecture.md (which has no such link).
findings.finding: The Remediation section directs the implementer to check only 'docs/architecture.md' for dangling references to bash-migration.md, but the actual inbound link set is much larger: README.md:604, nine src/rebar/ source files (module-level docstrings in _lib_writes.py, _engine_support/validate.py, _engine_support/next_batch.py, _store/lock.py, _commands/composer.py, _commands/leaf.py, _commands/__init__.py, _commands/unlink.py, _commands/_seam.py), and three test files (tests/integration/test_store_concurrency.py:1, tests/interfaces/queries/test_next_batch_compute.py:1, tests/interfaces/facades/test_leaf_writes_parity.py:1). None of these are mentioned; an implementer following the Remediation text will leave the bulk of the references intact, violating AC1 ('no dangling references').
findings.location: Remediation section — 'Delete docs/bash-migration.md (confirm/remove any docs/architecture.md pointer)'
findings.suggested_fix: In the Remediation, replace 'confirm/remove any docs/architecture.md pointer' with: 'audit all files for bash-migration.md references (`grep -r bash-migration.md .`); update source-file and test-file docstrings to cite the archived path (docs/archive/ if the file is moved there) or to docs/architecture.md and docs/concurrency.md as the live successors; update README.md:604 similarly.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Re-locate the stale version string that needs updating in `src/rebar/config.py`: line 442 contains no version marker; grep the file for '0.6.0' or 'Release:' to find the real line and update the plan's citation before execution.
findings.finding: The plan asserts a stale 'Release: 0.6.0' marker at `src/rebar/config.py:442`, but reading that file shows line 442 is `cli_overrides: dict | None = None,` inside `_ordered_layers` — no version string exists there. A repo-wide grep for 'Release: 0.6.0' returns zero matches. The premise is false; the executor will search a non-existent target.
findings.suggested_fix: Run `grep -n '0.6.0\|Release:' src/rebar/config.py` to find the actual stale line and replace the `config.py:442` citation with the correct line number (or remove the AC clause if no such marker exists in config.py).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Locate the actual file and line containing the 'Release: 0.6.0' version marker (it does not exist in src/rebar/config.py:442); update the plan's edit target to the correct location or remove this sub-task if the stale marker doesn't exist.
findings.finding: The plan names 'src/rebar/config.py:442' as the location of a 'Release: 0.6.0' deprecation registry marker, but no such string exists anywhere in config.py. Line 442 is a function parameter declaration (`strict: bool = False,`); exhaustive search of config.py for '0.6.0', 'Release:', or any version marker returns zero matches. This edit target is hallucinated.
findings.location: Finding section / Remediation: 'Stale Release: 0.6.0 marker at src/rebar/config.py:442 (deprecation registry doc)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add docs/remediation-implementation-plan.md (7+ occurrences) and docs/releasing.md:213 as explicit edit targets for the ticket_txn.py → _commands/txn.py sweep, or narrow AC #2 to only 'event-schema.md cites _commands/txn.py' if a full docs/ sweep is not intended.
findings.finding: AC #2 requires 'no ticket_txn.py in docs/' after the fix, but the plan only targets event-schema.md. docs/remediation-implementation-plan.md contains at least 7 stale references to ticket_txn.py (lines 13, 42, 227, 317, 499, 523) and docs/releasing.md:213 also cites ticket_txn.py. These files are not named as edit targets.
findings.suggested_fix: Either update AC #2 to say 'event-schema.md updated' (scoped), or add remediation-implementation-plan.md and releasing.md to the edit list. Note: docs/bash-migration.md and docs/archive/ refs can be dropped when those files are deleted/archived.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify whether the 'Release: 0.6.0' marker at src/rebar/config.py:442 actually exists before the plan is executed; if not found, remove that sub-item from the Finding and AC3 so the implementer isn't chasing a ghost.
findings.finding: The plan cites a stale 'Release: 0.6.0' marker at src/rebar/config.py:442 and includes it in AC3, but no such marker exists there: config.py:442 is inside the _ordered_layers function body (no version string), and a full search of config.py and _deprecations.py finds zero occurrences of '0.6.0' or any release-version comment. The only confirmed 0.6.0 occurrence is api-stability.md:3. The ghost citation may cause the implementer to spend time searching a location that needs no edit, or to believe AC3 is satisfied after touching only api-stability.md while the config.py item remains 'open'.
findings.location: Finding section — 'Stale Release: 0.6.0 marker at src/rebar/config.py:442 (deprecation registry doc)'

findings.finding: No file_impact declared on a leaf work ticket.
findings.suggested_fix: Record the {path, reason} files this work will touch (e.g. via set_file_impact) so the attestation is scoped to them.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Resolve the 'confirm/remove' deferral: either state definitively that `docs/architecture.md` contains a link to `bash-migration.md` (and must have it removed) or that it does not (and can be ignored), so the executor has a clear, non-investigative action.
findings.finding: The remediation says 'confirm/remove any docs/architecture.md pointer' to bash-migration.md, which is a 'verify whether' placeholder deferring a real design decision (does the pointer exist and must it be removed?) to the executor.
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'confirm/remove any docs/architecture.md pointer' with either 'remove the bash-migration.md link from docs/architecture.md:LINE' (if known) or 'docs/architecture.md has no link to bash-migration.md — no change needed there' (if verified absent).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a brief note to CONTRIBUTING.md (or inline CI failure message) documenting that api-stability.md's version string must be kept in sync with pyproject.toml, and that the VERSION-SYNC CI job enforces this.
findings.finding: The new VERSION-SYNC CI guard introduces a new contributor contract (updating api-stability.md when bumping pyproject.toml version) but the plan does not call for documenting this constraint anywhere visible to contributors (e.g., CONTRIBUTING.md, CI failure message, or a comment in the guard script).
findings.location: Remediation section / AC line 5: 'CI fails if api-stability.md version diverges from pyproject.toml'
findings.suggested_fix: In the CI script or job definition, emit a human-readable failure message such as: 'ERROR: api-stability.md version does not match pyproject.toml. Update api-stability.md to reflect the new version.' Also add one sentence to CONTRIBUTING.md under the release/versioning section.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a concrete proving command for each AC, e.g.: AC1 `git ls-files docs/bash-migration.md | wc -l` → 0 AND `grep -rn 'bash-migration' docs/ README.md` → no output; AC2 `grep -rn 'ticket_txn' docs/` → no output; AC3 `grep '0.7.1' docs/api-stability.md src/rebar/config.py` → both match; AC4 `grep -E 'rebar explain|rebar idea' README.md` → two hits; AC5 temporarily set a wrong version in api-stability.md and confirm CI step exits non-zero.
findings.finding: No proving command is supplied for any of the five acceptance criteria; an executor cannot produce concrete evidence of completion without stopping to invent checks.
findings.suggested_fix:
Append a 'Proving Commands' section:
```
# AC1
git ls-files docs/bash-migration.md | wc -l   # expect 0
grep -rn 'bash-migration' docs/ README.md     # expect no output

# AC2
grep -rn 'ticket_txn' docs/                   # expect no output

# AC3
grep -E '0\.7\.1' docs/api-stability.md src/rebar/config.py  # expect 2 matches

# AC4
grep -cE 'rebar (explain|idea)' README.md     # expect 2

# AC5 (guard smoke-test)
# In CI script: diff <(grep -oP '(?<=version: )\S+' docs/api-stability.md) <(grep -oP '(?<=version = ")\S+(?=")' pyproject.toml) || exit 1
# Verify by temporarily setting api-stability.md to 0.6.0 and confirming the step fails.
```

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] (Advisory) Consider whether the VERSION-SYNC CI guard warrants its own follow-on ticket so doc fixes can ship immediately; if the team judges them tightly coupled enough (same session, same PR), keeping them together is fine.
findings.finding: The plan bundles two independently-releasable outcomes: (1) fixing existing documentation drift (a pure doc-edit sweep) and (2) adding a new VERSION-SYNC CI enforcement mechanism that prevents future drift. The CI guard is independently valuable, testable, and releasable without the doc sweep, and vice versa.
findings.location: Remediation section; AC line 5 ('CI fails if api-stability.md version diverges from pyproject.toml')

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit rollback/back-out note to the plan (e.g., 'recoverable via `git revert` / `git checkout HEAD~1 -- docs/bash-migration.md`') so the destructive step's reversibility is stated, not assumed.
findings.finding: The deletion of docs/bash-migration.md is an irreversible destructive step that is justified in the plan but has no explicit rollback/back-out path stated. T4 requires more than acknowledgement — it requires a reversibility mechanism.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a single sentence to the Remediation: 'This deletion is reversible via git history (`git checkout <sha> -- docs/bash-migration.md`) if an undocumented consumer surfaces post-merge.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the implementation approach for the VERSION-SYNC guard (e.g., use `python -c "import tomllib" ` to extract the canonical version from pyproject.toml and document the exact line pattern expected in api-stability.md) so the guard is robust and its assumptions are explicit.
findings.finding: The plan warns against 'brittle stale-name grep' for the old CI approach but leaves the implementation approach for the version-sync guard itself completely unspecified, risking an equally brittle grep/regex over the api-stability.md and pyproject.toml version strings.
findings.location: Remediation section: 'Add a small VERSION-SYNC CI guard that fails if api-stability.md's version diverges from pyproject.toml (skip the brittle stale-name grep)'
findings.suggested_fix: Use `python -c "import tomllib; d=tomllib.load(open('pyproject.toml','rb')); print(d['project']['version'])"` for pyproject.toml extraction, and document the expected format of the version line in api-stability.md (e.g., `currently X.Y.Z`) so the guard's regex is pinned to a known, stable format.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a positive rationale for the VERSION-SYNC CI guard: state that it should be an inline shell step in test.yml (matching the prompt-index and public-types drift gates) that greps the version string from pyproject.toml and asserts it matches the version quoted in api-stability.md.
findings.finding: The plan does not state a positive rationale for why the VERSION-SYNC CI guard is scoped to a version-string comparison only (rather than, e.g., a broader doc-freshness check or a pre-commit hook). The rationale given is purely exclusionary ('skip the brittle stale-name grep') rather than affirmative. Per G6 criterion 4, absence of a positive rationale for the chosen approach is a finding.
findings.location: Remediation section — 'Add a small VERSION-SYNC CI guard that fails if api-stability.md's version diverges from pyproject.toml (skip the brittle stale-name grep — deleting bash-migration.md makes it moot)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the Remediation: 'Implement as an inline bash step in test.yml (consistent with the prompt-index and public-types drift gates): extract `version = "..."` from pyproject.toml with grep/sed, extract the `currently X.Y.Z` string from api-stability.md, and fail with a descriptive error if they differ.'

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
